title: Immediate Metabolic Response to LPS in Monocytes
author: Brandt D Pence
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ufcetiw

steps.title: Turn on XFp analyzer to warm...
steps.instruction: Turn on XFp analyzer to warm up overnight Hydrate XFp sensor cartridge Add 200 μl XF calibration solution (included with FluxPak) to each well. Add 400 μl sterile PBS or H 2 O to each moat. Incubate overnight at 37 o C in non-CO 2 incubator.

steps.title: Seahorse DMEM Media Add 50 μl...
steps.instruction: Seahorse DMEM Media Add 50 μl pyruvate, 50 μl L-glutamine, 20 μl glucose to 5 ml Seahorse Base Medium. Sterile filter before use. Cell-Tak coated XFp plate. Prepare Cell-Tak. Add Cell-Tak and sodium hydroxide to 0.1 M sodium bicarbonate so that each well will receive 0.56 μg Cell-Tak, and sodium hydroxide concentration will be 0.63 mM. Cell-Tak concentration varies by batch, so calculations for each new batch will need to be performed. Add 25 μl Cell-Tak solution to each well. Plate can be stored at room temperature (at least 20 minutes) until use (for a few hours). Plates can also be prepped prior to assay day by incubating plates in Cell-Tak (at least 20 minutes), then aspirating remaining solution, air drying, and storing at 4 o C until use. Monocytes Monocytes should be prepared as directed in the monocyte isolation protocol. Isolated monocytes should be diluted to a concentration of 3×10 6 cells / ml in prepared Seahorse DMEM media prior to use in the assay. Use monocytes immediately.

steps.title: Aspirate Cell-Tak solution if not already done
steps.instruction: Aspirate Cell-Tak solution if not already done.

steps.title: Add 50 μl medium to wells...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μl medium to wells A and H and 50 μl cells to wells B-G. Samples are generally run in duplicate or triplicate on each plate. Cell number is 1.5×10 5 cells per well.

steps.title: Place plate in the carrier and place in centrifuge
steps.instruction: Place plate in the carrier and place in centrifuge. Spin 300× g for 1 minute without brake .

steps.title: Add 130 μl assay medium to...
steps.instruction: Add 130 μl assay medium to each well A-H (final volume 180 μl).

steps.title: Incubate plate at 37 o C...
steps.instruction: Incubate plate at 37 o C in non-CO 2 incubator for 1 hour.

steps.title: While plate is incubating, perform steps 9-12
steps.instruction: While plate is incubating, perform steps 9-12.

steps.title: Prepare LPS dilution Thaw 1 μg/ml...
steps.instruction: Prepare LPS dilution Thaw 1 μg/ml LPS stock Dilute 30 μl LPS stock in 270 μl assay medium (100 ng/ml)

steps.title: Remove sensor cartridge from incubator and...
steps.instruction: Remove sensor cartridge from incubator and remove and reinsert sensors briefly to clear air bubbles.

steps.title: Fill cartridge
steps.instruction: Fill cartridge: Port A (wells A-D, H): 20 μl assay medium ( media control condition ) Port A (wells E-G): 20 μl LPS dilution (10 ng/ml final concentration) The listed plate setup is for a comparison between LPS and media in triplicate. This can be varied depending on the research question. For example, we compare LPS responses +/- glucose in triplicate in this assay. We have also used media, LPS, and an inhibitor+LPS in duplicate in this assay.

steps.title: Set up program on Seahorse XFp...
steps.instruction: Set up program on Seahorse XFp and calibrate sensor cartridge ( remove lid ). Basal: 5 measurements Injection 1: 23 measurements

steps.title: After 1 hour cell incubation, remove...
steps.instruction: After 1 hour cell incubation, remove utility plate from XFp and insert cell plate ( remove lid ).

steps.title: After run is completed, image each...
steps.instruction: After run is completed, image each well by photomicroscopy or collect and isolate protein from each well to normalize cell numbers.

steps.title: Data can be analyzed in several ways
steps.instruction: Data can be analyzed in several ways. The magnitude of the ECAR response can be measured by max ECAR - min ECAR or simply by max ECAR . The overall response kinetics are best measured by area under the curve (trapezoidal method). An example assay is shown in Figure 1. Figure 1 . Example assay
